# SEO Audit — 2026-05-22

**Status:** Frozen snapshot. Do not edit findings retroactively. New observations
go in a fresh audit file (`audit-YYYY-MM-DD.md`). Mutable follow-up work lives
in `sprint-entity-authority.md`.

Author of audit: Claude (Opus 4.7) with Khang Tran, via `/grill-me` session
2026-05-22. Decisions that shape this audit are recorded verbatim in section 0
so a future reader can interpret the findings without re-litigating the
framing.

---

## 0. Context & goals (decided 2026-05-22)

This audit captures the external SEO state of three surfaces — npm package
page, GitHub repo page, deployed docs site at
`https://ngbrutalism.khangtran.dev` — as observable by an outside crawler. The
internal code state (PLAN.md decisions, `docs-seo-data.ts` content) is *not*
re-inventoried here; this audit measures whether the shipped artifact matches
what the code intended to emit.

### 0.1 Goals (additive, both in scope)

**Goal #1 — LLM entity answer.** When a user asks a live-retrieval engine
(Google AI Overview, Perplexity, Gemini, ChatGPT/Claude with web mode)
*"What is ng-brutalism?"*, the answer converges on the following three
canonical sentences in any wording close to them:

> **Lead (citable):** *"Ng Brutalism is a neo-brutalist Angular UI library by Khang Tran."*
>
> **Sentence 2 (rich):** *"Built with signals, zoneless change detection, and Tailwind v4."*
>
> **Sentence 3 (npm anchor):** *"It ships as `@ng-brutalism/ui` on npm."*

Out of scope for the 1-week horizon: ChatGPT / Claude *without* web tools.
Those rely on training-data snapshots and are not movable on this timescale.

**Goal #2 — Search ranking.** Within 1 week of remediation work, the deployed
site appears in Google organic results at the following positions in
**incognito**, US-English geo:

| Tier | Keywords | Threshold | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| **T1 — branded** | `ng-brutalism`, `ng brutalism`, `@ng-brutalism/ui` | **Top 3** | Unique branded term, no incumbent competitor |
| **T2 — long-tail** | `angular brutalism`, `neobrutalism angular`, `brutalist angular components`, `neo-brutalist angular ui library` | **Top 10 (page 1)** | Low-competition, high intent-match |
| **T3 — mid-comp** | `brutalist ui components`, `angular component library brutalist` | Indexed only | 1–3 month horizon, out of 1-week scope |
| **T4 — head terms** | `angular ui library`, `angular component library` | Indexed only | 12+ month horizon. PLAN.md §2.2 explicitly deprioritized these. Tracked for baseline, not actioned. |

### 0.2 Audit scope (decided 2026-05-22)

| Layer | In scope? | Method |
|---|---|---|
| a. Indexability (robots, sitemap, canonical, status) | ✓ | curl |
| b. Head metadata (title, description, OG, Twitter, theme-color, author) | ✓ | curl + HTML parse |
| c. Structured data (JSON-LD type, fields populated) | ✓ | curl + JSON parse; **validator: USER ACTION (interactive only)** |
| d. Visible identity (H1, hero copy, eyebrows) | ✓ | curl + visible-text extract |
| e. Off-site identity (GitHub About / topics, npm metadata, README rendering) | ✓ | GitHub API + npm registry JSON |
| f. AI/LLM surfaces (`llms.txt` content + parity with sitemap) | ✓ | curl |
| g. Search performance (rankings, GSC impressions) | Partial | **USER ACTION** for live ranks; GSC excluded (needs auth) |

### 0.3 Scoring rules (decided 2026-05-22)

**7-fact LLM checklist** (per engine, per surface):

| # | Fact | Why |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Name (`ng-brutalism` / `Ng Brutalism`) | Entity recognition |
| 2 | Category (`library` / `component library` / `UI library`) | What it is |
| 3 | Framework (`Angular`) | Disambiguates from React/Vue brutalist libs |
| 4 | Style (`neo-brutalist` / `neobrutalism` / `brutalist`) | Visual identity |
| 5 | Author (`Khang Tran`) | Stakeholder request |
| 6 | npm name (`@ng-brutalism/ui`) | Install path + discriminator |
| 7 | Tech hooks (any of `signals`, `zoneless`, `Tailwind v4`) | Optional bonus |

**Severity scale**:

- 🔴 **Critical** — blocks goal #1 or goal #2 directly
- 🟡 **Important** — degrades but doesn't block
- 🟢 **Nice-to-have** — polish

Effort: **S** (≤15 min), **M** (≤2 hr), **L** (≥2 hr).

Crawled 2026-05-22 11:00–11:30 ICT (UTC+7) from a developer machine via `curl`
(no proxy, no auth, no JavaScript execution — matches what Googlebot's first
fetch sees for an SSG site).

| # | Surface | URL | Fetched-at | HTTP | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

| 2 | npm public page | `https://www.npmjs.com/package/@ng-brutalism/ui` | 2026-05-22 | 200 | **Cloudflare bot challenge** returned — server-side curl gets a 5.4 KB "Just a moment…" shell, not real content. See §2.2. |
| 3 | GitHub API repo | `https://api.github.com/repos/khangtrannn/ng-brutalism` | 2026-05-22 | 200 | 6 KB JSON |
| 4 | GitHub API topics | `https://api.github.com/repos/khangtrannn/ng-brutalism/topics` | 2026-05-22 | 200 | 12 topics |
| 5 | GitHub repo page | `https://github.com/khangtrannn/ng-brutalism` | 2026-05-22 | 200 | 374 KB HTML — full server-rendered |
| 6 | GitHub README (root) | `raw.githubusercontent.com/.../main/README.md` | 2026-05-22 | 200 | 6.4 KB |
| 7 | npm-published README | `raw.githubusercontent.com/.../libs/ui/README.md` | 2026-05-22 | 200 | 6.6 KB; same content as #6, image URL absolute |
| 8 | Docs `/robots.txt` | `…/robots.txt` | 2026-05-22 | 200 | 79 B; allows all, names sitemap |
| 9 | Docs `/sitemap.xml` | `…/sitemap.xml` | 2026-05-22 | 200 | 3.8 KB; 19 URLs |
| 10 | Docs `/llms.txt` | `…/llms.txt` | 2026-05-22 | 200 | 4.3 KB; 19 entries, parity with sitemap ✓ |
| 11–29 | Docs site 19 pages | listed in §4 | 2026-05-22 | 200 (all) | Prerendered (Analog SSG). See §4. |

## 2. npm package

### 2.1 npm registry metadata (`registry.npmjs.org`)

Authoritative source. Crawlers and most LLM training pipelines consume this
JSON, not the JS-rendered `npmjs.com` page.

```
name:        @ng-brutalism/ui
description: Neo-brutalist Angular component library (ng-brutalism) with
             signals, zoneless, token-driven design, and Tailwind v4.
latest:      0.1.1
license:     MIT
homepage:    https://ngbrutalism.khangtran.dev
repository:  git+https://github.com/khangtrannn/ng-brutalism.git
author:      { name: "Khang Tran", email: "khangtrann8198@gmail.com" }
maintainers: [{ name: "khangtrann", email: "khangtrann8198@gmail.com" }]
created:     2026-05-21T10:30:25Z
modified:    2026-05-21T15:59:15Z
versions:    [0.1.0, 0.1.1]
```

Keywords (19):

`ng-brutalism`, `neo-brutalism`, `neo-brutalism-ui`, `neobrutalism`,
`neobrutalist`, `brutalism`, `brutalist-design`, `angular`, `angular-ui`,
`angular-components`, **`angular-brutalism`**, `ui`, `ui-library`,
`component-library`, `design-system`, `tailwind`, `tailwindcss`, `signals`,
`zoneless`.

**7-fact score for npm registry surface: 7/7** ✓

| Fact | Present | Where |
|---|---|---|
| Name | ✓ | `name: @ng-brutalism/ui` + `description` mentions "ng-brutalism" |
| Category | ✓ | "component library" in description; `ui-library` in keywords |
| Framework | ✓ | "Angular" in description; `angular` + `angular-ui` keywords |
| Style | ✓ | "Neo-brutalist" in description; `neo-brutalism`, `neobrutalist`, `brutalism` keywords |
| Author | ✓ | `author.name: "Khang Tran"` — **only crawled surface where this appears in structured form** |
| npm name | ✓ | `name` field |
| Tech hooks | ✓ | "signals, zoneless… Tailwind v4" in description |

### 2.2 npm public page (`npmjs.com/package/@ng-brutalism/ui`)

**🔴 Finding NPM-1: Server-side curl returns a Cloudflare bot challenge, not
the package page.** Response is a 5.4 KB shell with `<title>Just a moment…</title>`
and `<meta name="robots" content="noindex,nofollow">`. The "real" page renders
client-side after the JS challenge resolves.

Effect on crawlers:
- **Googlebot:** has cooperative bypass with Cloudflare; sees real content.
- **Most LLM training pipelines:** they fetch via simple HTTP without JS — they
  may receive the same challenge page and skip indexing.
- **Perplexity / Gemini / etc. at retrieval time:** depends on each engine's
  fetcher. Many use browser-mode and bypass; some don't.

**Mitigation already in place:** registry JSON (§2.1) carries the same content
in structured form. LLMs that consume the npm registry API (most do) get
correct data. **Not actionable in this audit** — it's npm's infrastructure,
not ours. Flagged for awareness only.

### 2.3 README (as published to npm)

Source: `libs/ui/README.md`, 6.6 KB. First lines:

> # ng-brutalism — Neo-brutalist Angular UI Component Library
>
> Build loud. Stay sharp.
>
> ng-brutalism is a neo-brutalist Angular UI component library — token-driven,
> signals-first, zoneless, with directive APIs, keyboard-ready interactions, and
> Tailwind v4 ergonomics from the first import.

**7-fact score for README (visible-to-readers surface): 6/7**

| Fact | Present |
|---|---|
| Name | ✓ |
| Category | ✓ "UI Component Library" |
| Framework | ✓ "Angular" |
| Style | ✓ "Neo-brutalist" |
| **Author** | ✗ **"Khang Tran" not mentioned in the README opening or anywhere in body text** |
| npm name | ✓ `@ng-brutalism/ui` |
| Tech hooks | ✓ "signals, zoneless, Tailwind v4" |

**🔴 Finding NPM-2: README does not contain the author name "Khang Tran" anywhere
in human-readable text.** The npm registry JSON has `author.name`, but the
README — which is what renders on the npm public page and gets crawled by
content-scrapers — does not.

```
updated:     2026-05-22T00:57:26Z
pushed:      2026-05-21T17:58:41Z
```

### 3.2 Topic tags (12)

`angular`, `angular-components`, `angular-ui`, `brutalist-design`,
`component-library`, `design-system`, `neo-brutalism`, `neobrutalism`,
`signals`, `tailwindcss`, `ui-library`, `zoneless`

Compared with PLAN.md §2.6 recommended set (12): identical. **Phase 6 manual
work shipped.** ✓

**7-fact score for `description + topics` surface: 4/7**

| Fact | Present |
|---|---|
| Name | ✗ — description says "Neo-brutalist Angular component library…" but never says **"ng-brutalism"** by name |
| Category | ✓ "component library" + `component-library` / `ui-library` topics |
| Framework | ✓ "Angular" + `angular` topic |
| Style | ✓ "Neo-brutalist" + `neo-brutalism` topic |
| **Author** | ✗ "Khang Tran" not in description |
| **npm name** | ✗ `@ng-brutalism/ui` not in description |
| Tech hooks | ✓ "Tailwind v4" |

**🔴 Finding GH-1: GitHub repo description is missing 3 of 7 canonical facts** —
the project name, the author, and the npm package name. Description has 92
characters used of 350 allowed. Significant headroom.

### 3.3 GitHub repo page HTML (`github.com/khangtrannn/ng-brutalism`)

Phrase counts in the rendered HTML (383 KB):

| Phrase | Count |
|---|---|
| `neo-brutalist` | 25 |
| `@ng-brutalism/ui` | 38 |
| `Khang Tran` | **0** |
| `khangtrannn` (username) | many (shown as the owner) |
| JSON-LD blocks | **0** (GitHub doesn't emit JSON-LD on repo pages) |

The `og:image` is a dynamic GitHub social card
(`opengraph.githubassets.com/.../khangtrannn/ng-brutalism`) showing repo name +
description + star/fork counts. That image is what gets pulled when the GitHub
URL is shared on social — not the docs site's `/og.png`.

**🟡 Finding GH-2: Khang Tran's full name does not appear on the repo page** —
only the GitHub username `khangtrannn`. Visitors and crawlers see the username
in the breadcrumb (`khangtrannn / ng-brutalism`) but no full-name string. For
LLM entity attribution, the user's GitHub profile page is the canonical source
of the name → username link, but the repo page does not assert it.

### 3.4 Root README (`README.md`)

Same opening as `libs/ui/README.md` (see §2.3). **Same gap: no "Khang Tran" string.**

**🔴 Finding GH-3: Neither root README nor the lib README mentions "Khang Tran".**
This is the highest-leverage single edit in the entire audit: adding *"created
by Khang Tran"* to the README opening would propagate to:
- GitHub repo page rendering (README is rendered inline on the repo page)
- npm package page (README is what renders on `npmjs.com/package/@ng-brutalism/ui`)
- Anyone fetching `raw.githubusercontent.com/.../README.md` directly
- LLM training scrapes that key off README content

## 4. Docs site (`ngbrutalism.khangtran.dev`)

### 4.1 robots / sitemap / llms.txt

**`robots.txt`** (79 bytes):

```
User-agent: *
Allow: /

Sitemap: https://ngbrutalism.khangtran.dev/sitemap.xml
```

Allows all, declares sitemap. ✓ Minimal, correct.

**`sitemap.xml`**: 19 URLs, all with `<lastmod>` timestamps in `2026-05-21`
range. `<priority>` and `<changefreq>` set per entry. No `/docs/<component>/`
duplicate entries (PLAN.md decision 2.3 shipped ✓).

**`llms.txt`** (4.3 KB): 19 URL entries with title + description per page.
**Parity with sitemap: 19/19** ✓. Opens with:

> # Ng Brutalism
>
> > The neo-brutalist Angular UI library. Signals, zoneless change detection, Tailwind v4. Bold borders, offset shadows, punchy colors.

**7-fact score for llms.txt: 6/7**

| Fact | Present |
|---|---|
| Name | ✓ |
| Category | ✓ |
| Framework | ✓ |
| Style | ✓ |
| **Author** | ✗ no "Khang Tran" string |
| npm name | ✓ |
| Tech hooks | ✓ |

**🔴 Finding DOCS-1: `llms.txt` does not mention Khang Tran.** This file is
specifically optimized for LLM consumption — if any single text file should
carry the canonical author attribution, it's this one.

### 4.2 Fully expanded: `/` (home)

```
HTTP:            200 (prerendered SSG)
Title:           "Ng Brutalism — Neo-Brutalist Angular UI Library"
Canonical:       https://ngbrutalism.khangtran.dev/
Description:     "The neo-brutalist Angular UI library. Signals, zoneless,
                  Tailwind v4. Bold borders, offset shadows, and punchy
                  colors — drop in brutalist Angular components and ship loud."
og:type:         website
og:title:        "Ng Brutalism — Neo-Brutalist Angular UI Library"
og:description:  (same as description)
twitter:card:    summary_large_image (from index.html static block)
robots:          index, follow
author:          Khang Tran                          ← present in <meta>, not visible to users
theme-color:     #ffd24a
GSV:             lq5mTAkarsSjy3r_0qKiuAUUvzEFyaXagIKcCZMmcd4   (home-only, correct per PLAN §2.6 ✓)
JSON-LD:         SoftwareApplication (1 block, validates as well-formed JSON)
H1:              "The neo-brutalism  UI library  for  Angular"
                      ↑
                  Notice: "neo-brutalism" not "neo-brutalist" — adjective form is awkward
First paragraph: "Ng Brutalism is the neo-brutalist Angular UI library —
                  a token-driven set of brutalist Angular components with
                  signals, zoneless-friendly change detection, and
                  Tailwind v4 ergonomics."
```

**SoftwareApplication JSON-LD (verbatim):**

```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "SoftwareApplication",
  "name": "Ng Brutalism",
  "alternateName": ["ng-brutalism", "@ng-brutalism/ui"],
  "description": "Neo-brutalist Angular UI library with signals, zoneless change detection, and Tailwind v4. Brutalist Angular components with hard borders and offset shadows.",
  "url": "https://ngbrutalism.khangtran.dev",
  "applicationCategory": "DeveloperApplication",
  "operatingSystem": "Web",
  "programmingLanguage": "TypeScript",
  "softwareVersion": "0.1.1",
  "license": "https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT",
  "codeRepository": "https://github.com/khangtrannn/ng-brutalism",
  "downloadUrl": "https://www.npmjs.com/package/@ng-brutalism/ui",
  "keywords": "neobrutalism angular, neo-brutalist angular ui library, brutalist angular components, angular component library, tailwindcss, signals, zoneless",
  "sameAs": ["https://www.npmjs.com/package/@ng-brutalism/ui",
             "https://github.com/khangtrannn/ng-brutalism",
             "https://github.com/khangtrannn"],
  "offers": { "@type": "Offer", "price": "0", "priceCurrency": "USD" },
  "author": { "@type": "Person", "name": "Khang Tran",
              "sameAs": ["https://github.com/khangtrannn"] }
}
```

**7-fact score for `/` (visible text: title + description + h1 + first paragraph): 6/7**

| Fact | Present in visible text |
|---|---|
| Name | ✓ |
| Category | ✓ "UI library" |
| Framework | ✓ |
| Style | ✓ |
| **Author** | ✗ **"Khang Tran" is in `<meta name="author">` but never appears in visible body text on `/`** |
| npm name | ✓ `@ng-brutalism/ui` in install snippet visible text |
| Tech hooks | ✓ |

**🔴 Finding DOCS-2: Home page has no visible author attribution.** The
`SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD has `author.name: "Khang Tran"` — Google AI
Overview and structured-data-aware engines will pick it up. But for LLMs that
key off visible text (most do, as a strong signal), the home page never
asserts authorship. Adding a single visible "by Khang Tran" line — likely
right under the H1 — fixes this.

**🟡 Finding DOCS-3: Home H1 wording is `"The neo-brutalism UI library for Angular"`.**
Adjective form would be cleaner: *"The neo-brutalist Angular UI library."* The
current form mixes the noun "neo-brutalism" with the noun phrase "UI library"
— grammatically awkward for both readers and tokenizers that key off adjective
patterns. Single word change.

```

Canonical:       /docs/installation/ ✓
Description:     "Install @ng-brutalism/ui in your Angular project. Requires a
                  modern Angular setup, Tailwind v4, and Node 20+."
og:type:         article ✓
JSON-LD:         BreadcrumbList + TechArticle ✓
H1:              "Installation"
GSV:             absent (correct — home-only per PLAN.md §2.6 ✓)
```

**7-fact score (title + description): 4/7** — misses style ("neo-brutalist"
not in description) and author. Acceptable for an install-focused page; not a
critical surface for entity recognition.

### 4.4 Fully expanded: `/components/button/`

```
Title:           "Neo-Brutalist Angular Button | Ng Brutalism" ✓ canonical pattern
Canonical:       /components/button/ ✓
Description:     "Neo-brutalist Button component for Angular. Hard borders,
                  offset shadows, and multiple variants — built with directive

og:type:         article ✓

H1:              "Button"
Eyebrow:         "Neo-Brutalist Angular Component" (verified via PLAN.md §2.4 — shipped)
```

**7-fact score: 4/7** — no author, no `@ng-brutalism/ui`, no "library" string
(it's a per-component page; `library` belongs upstream, not here). Acceptable.

### 4.5 Fully expanded: `/showcase/portfolio/`

```
Title:           "Portfolio Showcase | Ng Brutalism"
Canonical:       /showcase/portfolio/ ✓
Description:     "Portfolio showcase built with Ng Brutalism — see the
                  neo-brutalist Angular UI library in action."
og:type:         article
JSON-LD:         BreadcrumbList only (NO TechArticle)
H1:              "I'm Khang Tran. 👋"   ← only page with author name in H1!
```

**🟡 Finding DOCS-4: `/showcase/portfolio/` has `og:type: article` but no
`TechArticle` JSON-LD.** `docs-title-strategy.ts` only emits `TechArticle` for
paths starting with `/docs/` or `/components/`. Showcase falls through. Either
add it (consistent with the og:type), or change og:type to `website` so the
two signals agree. Low-priority; affects rich-result eligibility for the
showcase page only.

**Interesting positive signal:** This is the *only* crawled surface where the
H1 contains "Khang Tran" verbatim. If LLM training data scrapes this page,
the entity link (ng-brutalism ↔ this page ↔ "Khang Tran") forms.

**Net:** all 15 component pages + 2 docs pages follow the pattern. No
unexpected deviations. PLAN.md §2.4 fully shipped. ✓

## 5. LLM probe baseline (USER ACTION)

The following engines need to be queried from your browser (logged-out / incognito

## 6. Ranking baseline (USER ACTION)

Server-side rank tracking is blocked (DuckDuckGo and Google return anti-bot
challenges to scripted clients). The user runs incognito searches from a real
browser and pastes the position.

For each keyword: open Google in a fresh incognito window (US-English), search
the keyword, and record the position where `ngbrutalism.khangtran.dev`,
`github.com/khangtrannn/ng-brutalism`, or `npmjs.com/package/@ng-brutalism/ui`
appears. "Top 30" means within the first 3 result pages; "Not visible" means
not within top 30.

| Tier | Keyword | Position 2026-05-22 | Top result domain |
|---|---|---|---|
| T1 | `ng-brutalism` | | |
| T1 | `ng brutalism` | | |
| T1 | `@ng-brutalism/ui` | | |
| T2 | `angular brutalism` | | |
| T2 | `neobrutalism angular` | | |
| T2 | `brutalist angular components` | | |
| T2 | `neo-brutalist angular ui library` | | |
| T3 | `brutalist ui components` | | |
| T3 | `angular component library brutalist` | | |
| T4 | `angular ui library` | | |
| T4 | `angular component library` | | |

**Also check** `site:ngbrutalism.khangtran.dev` (count of indexed URLs):

```
[paste count]
```

Expected: 19 (matches sitemap).

---

## 7. Gap summary

### 7.1 Per-surface 7-fact scoreboard

| Surface | Score | Missing facts |
|---|---|---|
| npm registry JSON | 7/7 | — |
| npm README (`libs/ui/README.md`) | 6/7 | Author |
| GitHub repo description + topics | 4/7 | Name, Author, npm name |
| GitHub root README | 6/7 | Author |
| GitHub repo page HTML (rendered) | 6/7 | Author (only username shown) |
| Docs `/` (visible text) | 6/7 | Author |
| Docs `/` (SoftwareApplication JSON-LD) | 7/7 | — |
| Docs `/docs/introduction/` | 5/7 | Author, npm name |
| Docs `/docs/installation/` | 4/7 | Style, Category, Author |
| Docs `/components/button/` | 4/7 | Category, Author, npm name |
| Docs `/showcase/portfolio/` | 4/7 | Author (in H1 only), npm name, Tech hooks |
| Docs `llms.txt` | 6/7 | Author |

### 7.2 Findings, prioritized

| ID | Severity | Surface | Finding | Effort | Action ref |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **GH-3** | 🔴 Critical | GitHub + npm README | README opening does not mention "Khang Tran" anywhere in body text. Single highest-leverage edit — propagates to GitHub repo page, npm public page, raw README fetches, and LLM training scrapes. | S | SPRINT-1 |
| **DOCS-2** | 🔴 Critical | Docs `/` | Home page has no visible "Khang Tran" attribution in body text. JSON-LD has it; visible text does not. | S | SPRINT-2 |
| **GH-1** | 🔴 Critical | GitHub `description` | 92/350 chars used; missing project name "ng-brutalism", author "Khang Tran", and `@ng-brutalism/ui` npm pointer. | S | SPRINT-3 |
| **DOCS-1** | 🔴 Critical | Docs `llms.txt` | LLM-facing manifest does not mention author. | S | SPRINT-4 |
| **DOCS-3** | 🟡 Important | Docs `/` H1 | "The neo-brutalism UI library for Angular" — adjective form would read cleaner: "The neo-brutalist Angular UI library." | S | SPRINT-5 |
| **GH-2** | 🟡 Important | GitHub repo page | "Khang Tran" full name absent from rendered HTML; only username `khangtrannn` shown. Out-of-band fix: full name shows in profile and in commit author fields rendered as text. | S | SPRINT-6 |
| **DOCS-4** | 🟡 Important | Docs `/showcase/portfolio/` | og:type=`article` but no `TechArticle` JSON-LD; pick one or the other so signals agree. | S | SPRINT-7 |
| **NPM-1** | 🟢 Informational | npm public page | Cloudflare bot challenge blocks server-side curl. Not actionable; flagged for awareness. | — | — |
| **NPM-2** | 🔴 Critical (merges with GH-3) | npm-published README | Same root cause as GH-3 — same README, same gap. Fix once. | — | covered by SPRINT-1 |

### 7.3 Headline observation

The audit's central finding, stated plainly:

> **The string "Khang Tran" appears in structured data (npm registry, docs JSON-LD)
> but not in the visible body text or H1 of any primary crawled surface except
> `/showcase/portfolio/`.** Every other gap is secondary to this one. LLMs and
> search engines that score visible-text signals heavily — i.e. all of them —
> cannot reliably attribute authorship from the current external state.

If a future SEO audit reports "LLMs say 'ng-brutalism is a library' but don't
name the author," this is why.

---

## 8. Audit hand-off

- **Next step:** see `sprint-entity-authority.md` (this folder) for the
  prioritized punch list of edits derived from §7.
- **Re-audit cadence:** suggested 60–90 days post-deploy of sprint items, to
  give Google + LLM retrieval indexes time to refresh. Capture as
  `audit-2026-MM-DD.md`.
- **What to compare across audits:** §7.1 scoreboard table + §5.7 LLM probe
  scores + §6 ranking baseline. Each is a frozen-in-time vector; the diff
  across audits is the SEO signal.
